title: MCAT question archetype catalog (agent-iterable)
note: Companion to docs/01_mcat_question_taxonomy.md. Each concrete MCAT item = (skill) x (content) x (surface form). Families collapse the AAMC Scientific Inquiry & Reasoning Skills (SIRS) into generation buckets; archetypes are the named, generation-ready combinations an agent iterates over. Weights are AAMC's published per-section SIRS distribution (identical across C/P, B/B, P/S). 'expert_feel' and 'note_seedability' are 0-1 design heuristics for scope selection, not AAMC data.

sections.name: Chemical & Physical Foundations of Biological Systems
Critical Analysis & Reasoning Skills
sections.reason_out_of_scope: requires humanities/social-science passages, not medical notes

sections.name: Biological & Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems
Psychological, Social & Biological Foundations of Behavior

families.name: Knowledge & Discrimination
families.essence: recognize/recall/relate a concept or plug into a given equation

families.name: Applied Reasoning
families.essence: use a concept to predict, explain, or judge a novel case

families.name: Research & Experiment
families.essence: evaluate methodology: variables, controls, confounds, fixes, ethics

families.name: Data & Statistics
families.essence: read a table/graph/result; draw the conclusion the data do and do not support

passage_types.note: study setup, methods, variables, results/tables/figures; richest soil for expert questions
phenomenon/model/real-world situation to interpret and apply
advances a claim; evaluate the argument (more common in P/S)

archetypes.shape: short scenario instantiating one concept; ask which process/principle it is; distractors = the confusable neighbors

good_example.stem: Repeated drops of lemon juice produce declining salivation; the researcher then switches to lime juice. This most likely studies which process?

archetypes.llm_failure: names the concept in the stem, or draws distractors from unrelated chapters so topic-matching gives it away

archetypes.shape: give one representation, ask for the matching representation

good_example.stem: Which graph best shows the relationship between educational attainment and life expectancy?

archetypes.llm_failure: trivial one-to-one mapping with no plausible misreadings
difficulty_levers: how similar the decoy graphs are

archetypes.input_needed: a mechanism/pathway with a control point (enzyme kinetics, feedback loop, circuit, equilibrium)
archetypes.shape: introduce a perturbation (inhibitor, mutation, dpH, added resistance); ask for the downstream consequence

good_example.stem: A pyruvate analog raises apparent Km for pyruvate from 0.10 to 0.55 mM with Vmax nearly unchanged. Which mechanism best describes its effect?
good_example.answer: Competitive inhibition with respect to pyruvate
distractors_rationale: uncompetitive (would lower both Km and Vmax)

archetypes.llm_failure: perturbation and answer are the same fact restated; distractors random rather than each a distinct mechanistic signature

archetypes.input_needed: a governing equation/relationship + a scenario where the target quantity is not the one directly given
archetypes.shape: novel scenario -> select the correct model -> compute or estimate

good_example.stem: Given aortic radius/velocity and capillary radius/velocity, estimate the number of capillaries in the body.

archetypes.llm_failure: plug-and-chug where the needed equation is obvious and numbers are clean; no unit/order-of-magnitude reasoning

archetypes.input_needed: a condition with a recognizable sign/symptom/lab signature + differentials that share features
archetypes.shape: present symptoms/labs/history -> ask most likely diagnosis, underlying mechanism, or best next step

good_example.stem: A patient presents with fatigue, hyperpigmentation, hyponatremia, hyperkalemia, and hypotension. The primary defect most likely lies in the:
distractors_rationale: pituitary (would not cause hyperkalemia; ACTH-independent clues withheld)

archetypes.llm_failure: pathognomonic giveaway spelled out in the stem; differentials share no presenting feature (classic 'on-the-nose' item)

archetypes.input_needed: a theory/model with predictive content (cultural capital, Maslow, Le Chatelier, natural selection)
archetypes.shape: which outcome does <theory> predict in <new situation>?

good_example.stem: What does the concept of cultural capital predict?
good_example.answer: Cultural distinctions associated with elite classes will be more valued within a society.

archetypes.llm_failure: restates the theory's definition as the correct option

archetypes.input_needed: a describable study with manipulated and measured quantities
archetypes.shape: describe the study -> ask for IV / DV / control / confound

good_example.stem: Participants prepared a report alone or in a group; researchers tracked each person's contribution. What are the IV and DV?
good_example.answer: IV = alone vs group; DV = individual contribution
distractors_rationale: name task-type as IV (a held-constant factor)
name group productivity as DV (aggregate, not per-person)

archetypes.llm_failure: IV/DV are labeled in the stem; confound options are nonsense
difficulty_levers: ask for confound/control instead of IV/DV

archetypes.input_needed: a method with a realistic failure mode / confound
archetypes.shape: present a method with a subtle problem -> ask what change removes the confound (or what flaw invalidates the conclusion)

good_example.stem: A urinary-protein precipitation test gives false positives from calcium phosphate. Which procedure prevents precipitation of the salt?
good_example.answer: Add buffer to maintain neutral pH
distractors_rationale: add calcium hydroxide (adds the interfering ion)
add sodium phosphate (adds the interfering ion)

archetypes.llm_failure: invents a flaw that is not real, or the fix is generic ('use a bigger sample')
difficulty_levers: domain knowledge needed to see it

archetypes.shape: which experiment/measurement best tests <hypothesis>? or which is the most appropriate control?

good_example.stem: To test whether an enzyme's rate boost is catalytic rather than due to substrate depletion, the best control is to:
good_example.answer: run the assay with heat-denatured enzyme
distractors_rationale: double substrate (changes the variable of interest)
add product (tests inhibition, not the claim)

archetypes.llm_failure: the 'best' design is obviously best; alternatives do not actually test the hypothesis
difficulty_levers: how close the decoy designs come to working

archetypes.input_needed: a quantitative relationship expressible as a small table/graph
archetypes.shape: present a compact data object -> ask the single conclusion it supports

good_example.stem: Hemoglobin O2-binding curves are shown at pH 7.2, 7.4, 7.6. What conclusion can be drawn?
good_example.answer: Low pH favors the low-affinity oxygen-binding state.
distractors_rationale: affinity independent of pH (ignores the shift)
noncooperative at low pH (misreads sigmoid shape)

archetypes.llm_failure: the 'data' merely restate a fact; distractors not tied to specific misreadings; correct answer over-generalizes beyond the data
difficulty_levers: distractors that over-claim beyond the data

archetypes.input_needed: a theory/model AND a plausible follow-up finding
archetypes.shape: state a theory -> introduce a NEW study result -> ask what it does to the theory (supports / weakens / is consistent-but-not-diagnostic / requires which revision)

good_example.stem: Model M predicts trait X arises from mechanism A. A follow-up study finds X still appears when A is blocked, but only under condition Z. The finding is best interpreted as:
good_example.answer: evidence that A is not necessary for X (the model needs revision), but only within condition Z
distractors_rationale: fully confirms M (ignores the blockade result)
consistent but uninformative (under-claims; the blockade IS informative)

archetypes.llm_failure: the new study trivially confirms or trivially refutes; no 'consistent-but-uninformative' trap; the stated conclusion is not actually licensed by the finding

archetypes.input_needed: a result with a statistical property (correlation, p-value, CI, spread, sampling frame)
archetypes.shape: ask what claim the statistics license (correlation!=causation, CI meaning, median vs mean, generalizability)

good_example.stem: Which correlation supports the bystander effect?
good_example.answer: Number of bystanders is positively correlated with time-to-help.

archetypes.llm_failure: conflates significance with effect size; distractors are not real statistical misconceptions

overlays.rule: 3 statements (I/II/III); options are non-redundant combinations; each statement must be independently defensible
overlays.failure: one statement trivially true/false makes combinations collapse

overlays.rule: 3 options true, 1 false (or 'least' supported); the odd-one-out must not be obvious
overlays.failure: the false option is from a different category, giving it away

overlays.rule: choose the claim AND the correct 'because' reason; both halves independently gradeable
overlays.failure: a right claim paired only with right reason (no true/false crossing)

overlays.rule: 'According to Figure/Table...'; answer must be forced by the data object, not prior knowledge
overlays.failure: answerable from memory without the figure

quality_checks.rule: tested idea is applied in a context NOT stated in the source note
stem is answerable before reading the options
every distractor maps to a specific, writable misconception or reasoning error
same category, grammar; correct answer not the longest; length variance bounded
reject options matching /all of the above|none of the above/i
F3/F4 items should sometimes make 'cannot be concluded / not diagnostic' the correct answer
no grammatical agreement leak, absolute-word giveaways, or lone round-number answer
exactly one option is fully correct; each distractor fully incorrect or clearly inferior

generation_loop: 1. pick a concept from content_vocabulary_ref (optionally seeded by a first-principles card)
2. pick an archetype (weighted by the Deliverable-3 scope decision)
4. generate stem + 4 options + per-choice rationale + answer, following 'shape'
5. self-filter against quality_checks (drop on any disqualifying failure)
6. keep survivors; log the (concept, archetype, overlay) tuple for coverage tracking
